question: Provide documentation of your organizational mission and how it informs cybersecurity risk management decisions (e.g. mission statement linked to risk strategy).
Submit evidence of identified internal and external stakeholders and their cybersecurity expectations (stakeholder register or requirements matrix).
Provide your register of legal, regulatory, and contractual cybersecurity requirements with assigned owners.
Provide documentation of outcomes, capabilities, and services that the organization depends on (dependency/BIA register).
Submit your risk management strategy document showing risk objectives approved by leadership.
Provide evidence of established and communicated risk appetite and risk tolerance statements.
Demonstrate a documented strategic direction for risk response (acceptance, mitigation, transfer, avoidance) with an example decision.
Provide evidence that leadership is accountable for cybersecurity risk (e.g. board/exec charter or meeting minutes assigning accountability).
Submit documentation defining cybersecurity roles, responsibilities, and authorities (RACI) and evidence of communication.
Demonstrate that adequate resources (budget, staffing, tooling) are allocated to cybersecurity (budget approval or staffing plan).
Submit your organizational cybersecurity policy showing leadership approval, version, and communication evidence.
Provide evidence that policies are reviewed and updated to reflect changes (review log with dates and rationale).
Demonstrate that cybersecurity risk strategy outcomes are reviewed (e.g. KRI dashboard reviewed by leadership).
Submit evidence that cybersecurity performance is measured and used to inform improvements (metrics report with actions taken).
Provide your cyber supply chain risk management strategy and program documentation approved by stakeholders.
Submit your supplier register prioritized by criticality with assigned risk tiers and last-assessment dates.
Provide evidence of ongoing supplier risk monitoring (assessment cadence records, SOC 2 review notes, or continuous-monitoring reports).
Demonstrate that suppliers are included in incident planning, response, and recovery (supplier notification clauses or joint-test evidence).
Provide an inventory export of hardware assets showing owner, location, and classification. Include MDM device inventory from Kandji.
Submit an inventory of software, services, and data repositories including version and support status.
Provide network communication and data flow diagrams for your in-scope environment.
Provide evidence that assets are prioritized by classification, criticality, and business value (asset criticality rating).
Submit your data inventory showing data types, locations, and classification across the lifecycle. Include Snowflake data catalog if applicable.
Provide your latest vulnerability assessment results showing identified vulnerabilities. Pull from EDR (CrowdStrike Spotlight) and SAST (Semgrep).
Demonstrate that cyber threat intelligence is received from information-sharing sources (feed config or sample threat intel report).
Submit your risk register showing likelihood, impact, and prioritization used to determine risk response.
Provide evidence that risk responses are chosen, prioritized, and tracked (risk treatment plan with owners and target dates).
Demonstrate that improvements are identified from evaluations (audit findings or assessment results tracked to remediation actions).
Provide evidence improvements are identified from security tests and exercises (penetration test or tabletop findings with remediation tracking).
Provide evidence that identities and credentials are managed throughout their lifecycle (joiner/mover/leaver report from Okta).
Provide evidence of MFA enforcement for remote access and privileged accounts (MFA enrollment report from Okta and AWS IAM).
Submit evidence of least-privilege and separation-of-duties enforcement (role definitions and a recent access review with remediation).
Provide security awareness training completion records for general staff for the current cycle.
Submit evidence of role-based specialized training for privileged users and developers (training records).
Provide evidence that data at rest is protected (encryption-at-rest configuration for S3, RDS, and Snowflake).
Demonstrate that data in transit is protected (TLS configuration scan of public endpoints, Cloudflare TLS settings, minimum TLS version enforcement).
Submit your backup configuration and evidence of a recent successful backup and restore test.
Provide your configuration/hardening baseline for a key platform and evidence of enforcement (Kandji blueprint config or CIS-benchmark scan results).
Demonstrate software is maintained and patched. Pull patch-compliance reports from Kandji (workstations) and provide server patching process evidence.
Provide evidence that logs are generated and available for monitoring (CloudTrail config, Okta syslog, GitHub audit log configuration and retention settings).
Provide evidence of a secure software development lifecycle (SDLC standard, Semgrep SAST in CI/CD pipeline, branch protections in GitHub, env0 deployment approvals).
Demonstrate networks are protected from unauthorized access (Cloudflare WAF/DDoS config, AWS security groups, firewall rules and segmentation diagram).
Provide evidence of resilience mechanisms to meet availability objectives (HA architecture, multi-AZ configuration, or failover test results).
Submit evidence that adequate resource capacity is maintained (cloud capacity monitoring dashboards with thresholds).
Provide evidence that networks and network services are monitored (Cloudflare analytics/alerts, AWS GuardDuty findings, network monitoring dashboard).
Provide evidence of personnel activity and technology usage monitoring (Okta anomaly detection reports, CrowdStrike behavioral alerts).
Provide evidence that endpoints and services are monitored for adverse events (CrowdStrike EDR coverage report and recent detections).
Demonstrate that potentially adverse events are analyzed (sample security investigation with analyst notes showing triage to resolution).
Provide evidence that event data is correlated from multiple sources (SIEM correlation rules or multi-source alert example).
Provide your incident response plan and evidence it is executed when an incident is declared (sample declared-incident record).
Submit evidence that incidents are categorized and prioritized (incident severity matrix applied to a real or tabletop ticket).
Provide evidence that root-cause analysis is performed for incidents (RCA document for a recent incident or tabletop exercise).
Provide evidence that internal and external stakeholders are notified of incidents per plan (notification log or sample communications).
Provide evidence that incidents are contained and eradicated (containment and remediation steps documented in an incident record).
Provide your recovery plan and evidence it was executed during an incident or test (recovery runbook plus execution record or DR test report).
Provide evidence that the integrity of backups and restored assets is verified before use (restore-validation record).
Provide evidence that recovery activities are communicated to internal and external stakeholders (recovery status updates or communications log).
